question: What is the author name of Working Effectively with Legacy Code book? Working Effectively with Legacy Code book refers to title = 'Working Effectively with Legacy Code';
orig_question: What is the gender of Agent 13 hero? Agent 13 hero refers to superhero_name = 'Agent 13';
orig_masked: What is the [C7] of [V1] [T9]? [V1] [T9] refers to [C28] = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]
evidence: Working Effectively with Legacy Code book refers to title = 'Working Effectively with Legacy Code';
gold_sql: SELECT T1.author_name FROM author AS T1 INNER JOIN book_author as T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id INNER JOIN book as T3 ON T2.book_id = T3.book_id WHERE T3.title = 'Working Effectively with Legacy Code'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.gender FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN gender AS T2 ON T1.gender_id = T2.id WHERE T1.superhero_name = 'Agent 13'

question: What is the publisher name of the game ID 58? game ID 58 refers to game.id = 58;
orig_question: What is the publisher name of the superhero ID 38? superhero ID 38 refers to superhero.id = 38;
orig_masked: What is the [C15] of the [C24] [V1]? [C24] [V1] refers to [T9].[C24] = [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C24]
gold_sql: SELECT T3.publisher_name FROM game as T1 INNER JOIN game_publisher as T2 ON T1.id = T2.game_id INNER JOIN publisher as T3 ON T2.publisher_id = T3.id WHERE T1.id = 58
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.publisher_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id WHERE T1.id = 38

question: Identify the region name of the competitor who weighed 214 kg.  weighed 214 kg refers to weight = 214
orig_question: Identify the race of the superhero who weighed 169 kg.  weighed 169 kg refers to weight_kg = 169
orig_masked: Identify the [C17] of the [T9] who [C29] [V1].  [C29] [V1] refers to [C29] = [V2]; [V1] is a value of the column [C29]; [V2] is a value of the column [C29]
gold_sql: SELECT T3.region_name FROM person as T1 INNER JOIN person_region as T2 ON T1.id = T2.person_id INNER JOIN noc_region as T3 ON T2.region_id = T3.id WHERE T1.weight = 214
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.race FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.race_id = T2.id WHERE T1.weight_kg = 169

question: What is the height of the tallest player? Indicate his name. tallest player refers to MAX(height);
orig_question: What is the height of the tallest player? Indicate his name. tallest player refers to MAX(height);
orig_masked: What is the [C122] of the [C122] [T4]? Indicate his [C126]. [C122] [T4] refers to MAX([C122]);
gold_sql: SELECT full_name FROM person ORDER BY height DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM Player ORDER BY height DESC LIMIT 1

question: What is the name of businesses with the highest score? highest score refers to MAX(score)
orig_question: What is the name of players with the highest potential? highest potential refers to MAX(potential)
orig_masked: What is the name of [T4] with the highest [C158]? highest [C158] refers to MAX([C158])
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM businesses as T1 INNER JOIN inspections as T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.score = (SELECT MAX(score) FROM inspections)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t1.player_name FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id ORDER BY t2.potential DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many actors were born after 1980? born after 1980 refers to strftime('%Y', Date of Birth) = '1980';
orig_question: How many players were born after 1990? born after 1990 refers to strftime('%Y', birthday) = '1990';
orig_masked: How many [T4] were [C121] after [V1]? [C121] after [V1] refers to strftime('%Y', [C121]) = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C121]
evidence: born after 1980 refers to strftime('%Y', Date of Birth) = '1980';
orig_evidence: born after 1990 refers to strftime('%Y', birthday) = '1990';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT ActorID) FROM actor WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', "Date of Birth") > '1980'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(id) FROM Player WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', birthday) > '1990'
note: REVIEWED: Evidence for the original question is pretty misleading.

question: How many establishments who have inspections in CHICAGO are eligible for fines?  violation.fine contains the data of fines
orig_question: How many accounts who have region in Prague are eligible for loans?  A3 contains the data of region
orig_masked: How many [T1] who have [T5] in [V1] are eligible for [T6]?  [C25] contains the data of [T5]; [V1] is a value of the column [C25]
evidence: violation.fine contains the data of fines
orig_evidence: A3 contains the data of region
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.license_no) FROM establishment AS T1 INNER JOIN inspection AS T2 ON T1.license_no = T2.license_no INNER JOIN violation AS T3 ON T2.inspection_id = T3.inspection_id WHERE T3.fine > 0 AND T1.city = 'CHICAGO'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T1.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T3.A3 = 'Prague'
note: REVIEWED: This one was very tricky because of the 'eligible' token. This was the best I could come up with

question: Count the female management employees.  management refers to position.positionID = 2; female refers to employee.gender = F;
orig_question: Count the good female superheroes.  good refers to alignment.id = 1; female refers to gender.id = 2;
orig_masked: Count the [C1] [C7] [T9].  [C1] refers to [C2] = [V1]; [C7] refers to [C8] = [V2];; [V1] is a value of the column [C2]; [V2] is a value of the column [C8]
evidence: management refers to position.positionID = 2; female refers to employee.gender = F;
orig_evidence: good refers to alignment.id = 1; female refers to gender.id = 2;
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ssn) FROM employee as T1 INNER JOIN position AS T2 ON T1.positionID = T2.positionID WHERE T2.positionID = 2 AND T1.gender = 'F'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN alignment AS T2 ON T1.alignment_id = T2.id INNER JOIN gender AS T3 ON T1.gender_id = T3.id WHERE T2.alignment = 'Good' AND T3.gender = 'Female'

question: What is the minimum salary of position that ssn 109-87-6544 has, and how is this employee doing with performance?
orig_question: What is the amount of debt that client number 992 has, and how is this client doing with payments?
orig_masked: What is the [C34] of [T6] that [C10] [V1] has, and how is this [T3] doing with [C39]?  ; [V1] is a value of the column [C10]
gold_sql: SELECT T2.minsalary, T1.performance FROM employee as T1 INNER JOIN position as T2 ON T1.positionID = T2.positionID WHERE T1.ssn = '109-87-6544'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T4.amount, T4.status FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id INNER JOIN account AS T3 on T2.account_id = T3.account_id INNER JOIN loan AS T4 ON T3.account_id = T4.account_id WHERE T1.client_id = 992

question: Give the author name and publisher of the book named Programming in C. publisher refers to publisher_name;
orig_question: Give the alignment and superpowers of the superhero named Atom IV. superpowers refers to power_name;
orig_masked: Give the [C1] and [T10] of the [T9] [C28] [V1]. [T10] refers to [C31];; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]
gold_sql: SELECT T3.author_name, T4.publisher_name FROM book as T1 INNER JOIN book_author AS T2 ON T1.book_id = T2.book_id INNER JOIN author as T3 ON T3.author_id = T2.author_id INNER JOIN publisher as T4 ON T1.publisher_id = T4.publisher_id WHERE T1.title = 'Programming in C'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T4.alignment, T3.power_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN superpower AS T3 ON T3.id = T2.power_id INNER JOIN alignment AS T4 ON T1.alignment_id = T4.id WHERE T1.superhero_name = 'Atom IV'
